LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, THIRD, AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION., OVERRULED AS TO THE FIFTH, SIXTH, AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION, AND SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION. AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND, AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFFS DO NOT ALLEGE FACTS TO SHOW A FIDUCIARY RELATIONSHIP WITH DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA AND IT IS UNLIKELY THAT FURTHER LEAVE TO AMEND CAN CURE THIS DEFECT. AS FOR THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO ALLEGE, WITH SUFFICIENT PARTICULARITY, ANY FACTS TO SUPPORT A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD AGAINST DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA AND IT IS UNLIKELY FURTHER LEAVE TO AMEND CAN CURE THIS DEFECT. THE FIFTH, SIXTH, AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE ADEQUATELY PLED. AS TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFFS ARE GIVEN LEAVE TO ALLEGE, IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, THEIR PERFORMANCE OR EXCUSE FOR NON-PERFORMANCE OF THE CONTRACT. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER BY E-MAIL TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR #5976

Argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the first, second, third, and fourth causes of action., overruled as to the fifth, sixth, and eighth causes of action, and sustained with leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action. Off Calendar Aug-13-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS COMPLAINANT MAHER MUHAWIEH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION (as to plaintiff Leon Rashed). Appearances: Carlos A. Alvarez and Benjamin R. Ehrhart of Steyer Lowenthan Boodrakas LLP (421.3400) for cross complainant and moving party; Adam Gruen (510.526.4949) for plaintiff Leon Rashed; Richard S. Busch of Law Offices of Richard S. Busch (805.222.4234) appearing via Court Call for defendant Merchants Bonding Company.

Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: motion for summary judgment is denied. Motion for summary adjudication granted in part and denied in part. Motion denied as to 1st cause of action (Elder Abuse) without prejudice to raising argument before the trial judge. Triable issue of fact raised whether there was a fraud, and whether defendant knew or should have known of the wrongdoing. (Tigner deposition). Motion denied as to 8th cause of action (declaratory relief). Triable issue of fact as to who remains the owner of the property. Motion granted as to punitive damages and remaining causes of action. Plaintiff does not address the 2nd, 4th, 5th and 6th causes of action in his opposition and no triable issue of fact raised. The 3rd cause of action fails because no fiduciary or confidential relationship essential to a claim for constructive fraud is established. The 7th cause of action for negligence fails because the buyer owed the sellers no duty. The knowledge of a dual agent does not impute to the principal. Rulings on evidentiary objections filed in Court and are not binding on the trial court. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
